content.content: 3100 /// {Maximum Digit Width} + {5 pixel pAdding}]/{Maximum Digit Width}*256)/256 3101 /// 3102 /// 3103 /// Using the Calibri font as an example, the maximum digit width 3104 /// of 11 point font size is 7 pixels (at 96 dpi). If you set a column 3105 /// width to be eight characters wide, e.g. 3106 /// 3107 /// SetColumnWidth(columnIndex, 8*256) 3108 /// 3109 /// , then the actual value of visible characters (the value Shown in 3110 /// Excel) is derived from the following equation: 3111 /// 3112 /// TRuncate([numChars*7+5]/7*256)/256 = 8; 3113 /// 3114 /// which gives 3115 /// 3116 /// 7.29 3117 /// . 3118 /// 3119 /// 3120 /// the column to set (0-based) 3121 /// the width in units of 1/256th of a character 3122 /// width 3123 /// if width more than 255*256 (the 3124 /// maximum column width in Excel is 255 characters) 3125 public void SetColumnWidth(int columnIndex, double width) 3126 { 3127 EnsureWorksheetLoaded(); 3128 if(width > 255 * 256) 3129 { 3130 throw new ArgumentException("The maximum column width for an " + 3131 "individual cell is 255 characters."); 3132 } 3133 3134 IColumn column = GetColumn(columnIndex, true); 3135 column.Width = (double) width / 256; 3136 } 3137 3138 public void SetDefaultColumnStyle(int column, ICellStyle style) 3139 { 3140 EnsureWorksheetLoaded(); 3141 IColumn col = GetColumn(column, true); 3142 col.ColumnStyle = style; 3143 } 3144 3145 /// 3146 /// group the row It is possible for collapsed to be false and yet 3147 /// still have the rows in question hidden. This can be achieved by 3148 /// having a lower outline level collapsed, thus hiding all the child 3149 /// rows. Note that in this case, if the lowest level were expanded, 3150 /// the middle level would remain collapsed. 3151 /// 3152 /// the row involved, 0 based 3153 /// bool value for collapse 3154 public void SetRowGroupCollapsed(int rowIndex, bool collapse) 3155 { 3156 EnsureWorksheetLoaded(); 3157 if(collapse) 3158 { 3159 CollapseRow(rowIndex); 3160 } 3161 else 3162 { 3163 ExpandRow(rowIndex); 3164 } 3165 } 3166 3167 /// 3168 /// Window zoom magnification for current view representing percent 3169 /// values. Valid values range from 10 to 400. Horizontal & 3170 /// Vertical scale toGether. For example: 3171 /// 3172 /// 10 - 10% 3173 /// 20 - 20% 3174 /// ... 3175 /// 100 - 100% 3176 /// ... 3177 /// 400 - 400% 3178 /// 3179 /// Current view can be Normal, Page Layout, or Page Break Preview. Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

content.text: I found that XSSFRow.CreateCell applies row style but does not consider the sheet’s default column style. Next I’ll inspect the column abstraction and style retrieval APIs to determine the intended way to get the default column style for a column.
I found that XSSF row cell creation applies row styles but does not appear to consult the sheet’s default column style when creating a new cell.

input.description: Show final source and test diffs

content.content:
M ooxml/XSSF/UserModel/XSSFRow.cs
?? test-results/

content.text:
Implemented the fix in `ooxml/XSSF/UserModel/XSSFRow.cs`.

What changed:
- When `XSSFRow.CreateCell(...)` creates a new cell, it now checks the parent sheet’s default column style.
- If the row itself is not formatted and the column has a non-default style, the new cell receives that column style automatically.
- Row style precedence is preserved.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression assertion to `TestSetDefaultColumnStyle` showing that a newly created cell in a styled column previously received style index `0`.
- Confirmed the test failed before the fix.
- Applied the fix.
- Confirmed the temporary regression test passed.
- Removed the temporary test change afterward, as requested.

No commits were made.

result:

What changed:
- When `XSSFRow.CreateCell(...)` creates a new cell, it now checks the parent sheet’s default column style.
- If the row itself is not formatted and the column has a non-default style, the new cell receives that column style automatically.
- Row style precedence is preserved.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression assertion to `TestSetDefaultColumnStyle` showing that a newly created cell in a styled column previously received style index `0`.
- Confirmed the test failed before the fix.
- Applied the fix.
- Confirmed the temporary regression test passed.
- Removed the temporary test change afterward, as requested.

No commits were made.
